dataSourceInstructions:
Healthcare ontology graph for entity lookups and relationship traversals. 12 entity types, 18 relationships.

ENTITY TYPES:
  Provider — healthcare provider with specialty, department, NPI (PK: provider_key, Display: display_name). SPECIALTY VALUES: Cardiology, Internal Medicine, Family Practice, Orthopedics, Neurology, Oncology, Pediatrics, etc.
  Patient — patient demographics with age, gender, insurance (PK: patient_key, Display: patient_id)
  Prescription — medication prescription (PK: prescription_key, Display: prescription_id)
  Medication — drug with class, therapeutic area (PK: medication_key, Display: medication_name)
  Claim — billing claim with status, denial info (PK: claim_key, Display: claim_id)
  Payer — insurance payer (PK: payer_key, Display: payer_name)
  Encounter — patient visit with type, LOS, charges (PK: encounter_key, Display: encounter_id)
  Diagnosis — ICD diagnosis code (PK: diagnosis_key, Display: icd_description)
  PatientDiagnosis — links patient to diagnosis (PK: fact_diagnosis_key)
  CommunityHealth — SDOH by zip code (PK: sdoh_key, Display: zip_code)
  MedicationAdherence — patient medication adherence (PK: patient_key + medication_key)
  Vitals — patient vital signs (PK: patient_key + encounter_key)

RELATIONSHIPS:
  Prescription → Provider (prescribedBy, provider_key) — which provider wrote the prescription
  Prescription → Medication (dispenses, medication_key) — which medication was prescribed
  Prescription → Payer (PrescriptionHasPayer, payer_key) — which payer covers the prescription
  Claim → Provider (submittedBy, provider_key) — which provider submitted the claim
  Claim → Payer (ClaimHasPayer, payer_key) — which payer received the claim
  Claim → Encounter (originatesFrom/billsFor, encounter_key) — which encounter generated the claim
  Encounter → Patient (involves, patient_key) — which patient had the encounter
  Encounter → Provider (serves, provider_key) — which provider conducted the encounter
  PatientDiagnosis → Diagnosis (affects/references, diagnosis_key) — which diagnosis
  PatientDiagnosis → Patient (involves, patient_key) — which patient
  Patient → CommunityHealth (livesIn, zip_code) — patient's community health profile
  MedicationAdherence → Patient (adherenceFor, patient_key) — adherence for which patient
  MedicationAdherence → Medication (adherenceMedication, medication_key) — adherence for which medication
  Vitals → Patient (vitalsTakenFor, patient_key) — vitals for which patient
  Payer → Patient (covers, patient_key) — which patients the payer covers

SPECIALTY-BASED QUERIES:
  To find prescribing patterns for a specialty:
  1. Query Provider entities WHERE specialty = '<specialty_name>' (e.g., 'Internal Medicine', 'Cardiology')
  2. For each provider, traverse BACKWARDS via prescribedBy to find Prescriptions
  3. From Prescriptions, traverse to Medication (dispenses), Patient (involves via Encounter), and Payer (PrescriptionHasPayer)
  4. Return: Provider name/specialty, Medication names, Patient counts, Payer names

  Example: 'Prescribing patterns for Internal Medicine providers'
  → Filter Provider WHERE specialty='Internal Medicine'
  → Traverse Prescription ← prescribedBy → Provider
  → For each Prescription, get Medication via dispenses, Patient via encounter, Payer via PrescriptionHasPayer

WHEN TO USE THIS SOURCE:
  ✓ 'Show me providers in Cardiology' → filter Provider by specialty
  ✓ 'What medications does Dr. Smith prescribe?' → Provider → Prescription → Medication
  ✓ 'Which payers cover prescriptions from Internal Medicine?' → Provider(specialty) → Prescription → Payer
  ✓ 'Show me a provider's patient panel' → Provider → Encounter → Patient
  ✓ 'Trace a claim end-to-end' → Claim → Provider, Payer, Encounter → Patient
  ✓ 'Which providers treat high-risk patients?' → Patient(high risk) → Encounter → Provider

WHEN NOT TO USE (use lakehouse SQL instead):
  ✗ 'How many claims were denied?' → aggregation
  ✗ 'Average readmission rate' → aggregation
  ✗ 'Top 10 providers by volume' → ranking/aggregation
userDescription: Healthcare ontology graph with 12 entity types and 18 relationships. Use for: (1) Entity lookups by ID, name, or SPECIALTY (e.g., 'providers in Internal Medicine'), (2) Relationship traversals — trace claims to providers/payers/patients, follow prescriptions to medications, map provider-payer networks, (3) Care pathway exploration — patient encounters, diagnoses, prescriptions, adherence, vitals, SDOH, (4) Pattern discovery — prescribing patterns by specialty, denied claims with reasons, non-adherent patients. Provider entities have a SPECIALTY property for filtering (Cardiology, Internal Medicine, etc.). Start with the entity closest to what the user asks about, then traverse outward.

elements.description: Healthcare provider with specialty (Cardiology, Internal Medicine, etc.), department, display_name, and NPI number
Patient with demographics, age, gender, insurance type, and zip code
Medication prescription with fill date, days supply, quantity, and cost
Medication with drug class, therapeutic area, generic name, and chronic flag
Billing claim with status, denial flag, denial reason, billed and paid amounts
Insurance payer with payer name and type (Commercial, Medicare, Medicaid)
Patient encounter with type, length of stay, charges, readmission risk
ICD diagnosis with code, description, category, and chronic flag
Links patient to diagnosis with type, sequence, and present-on-admission flag
SDOH profile by zip code: poverty rate, food desert, transportation, uninsured rate
Patient medication adherence with PDC score, category, gap days
Patient vital signs: BP, heart rate, BMI, oxygen saturation
Prescription → Provider (which provider wrote the prescription)
Prescription → Medication (which medication was prescribed)
Prescription → Payer (which payer covers the prescription)
Claim → Provider (which provider submitted the claim)
Claim → Payer (which payer received the claim)
Claim → Encounter (which encounter generated the claim)
Claim → Encounter (billing relationship)
Encounter → Patient (which patient had the encounter)
Encounter → Provider (which provider conducted the encounter)
PatientDiagnosis → Diagnosis (which diagnosis)
PatientDiagnosis → Diagnosis (diagnosis reference)
Patient → CommunityHealth (patient's community by zip code)
Payer → Patient (which patients the payer covers)
MedicationAdherence → Patient (adherence for which patient)
MedicationAdherence → Medication (adherence for which medication)
Vitals → Patient (vitals for which patient)
Encounter time/location relationship
